varieties.name: Navaho
varieties.traits: Erect, self-supporting canes 1-1.5m, thornless, hardy to -15°C
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields, 5-6 week harvest mid-season, ripens late spring in humid subtropical climates

varieties.name: Ouachita
varieties.traits: Very erect canes 1-1.2m, thornless, vigorous growth
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yields comparable to Apache, 5 week harvest, mid-season
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for humid subtropical climates subtropical climate. Low chill requirement 200-400hr ideal for Queensland. Heat tolerant. Disease resistant to rust, double blossom, anthracnose. Popular at U-pick farms in subtropical Florida. Performs exceptionally well in humid subtropical climates region.
varieties.availability: Stark Bro's, Daley's Fruit, major Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Arapaho
varieties.traits: Erect, self-supporting, thornless, first semi-erect thornless variety developed
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, 4 week harvest, earliest ripening thornless (10 days before Navaho)
varieties.growing_notes: Well-suited for humid subtropical climates. Chill requirement 400-500hr suitable for subtropical climate. Heat and humidity tolerant. Highly disease resistant to orange rust, rosette, anthracnose. Ripens mid-July to August in SE Queensland. Strong upright growth.
varieties.availability: Stark Bro's, Edible Landscaping, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Apache
varieties.yield_notes: Highest yielding thornless variety, 5-6 week harvest late season
varieties.growing_notes: Marginal for humid subtropical climates due to high chill (800-900hr) - better for higher elevations like Tamborine Mt (600m). Disease resistant to orange rust, rosette, anthracnose. May experience white druplet/sunburn in hot conditions. Excellent postharvest potential.

varieties.name: Osage
varieties.yield_notes: High yields, ripens mid-early slightly before Ouachita
varieties.growing_notes: Good for humid subtropical climates subtropical conditions. Stores and ships well commercially. Heat tolerant. Mid-season ripening suitable for Queensland production.

varieties.name: Chester
varieties.traits: Semi-erect, thornless, vigorous canes to 2.4m, requires trellis
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, late season ripening mid-August to September

varieties.name: Triple Crown
varieties.traits: Semi-erect, thornless, vigorous 1.2-1.5m, strong canes can support fruit without trellis
varieties.yield_notes: Extremely high - up to 13kg per plant, 30 day harvest late July-August, named for productivity, vigor, flavor
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for humid subtropical climates with low chill (300hr). Heat tolerant. Can grow in partial shade. Fast growing. Popular for PYO operations and home gardens. Semi-erect habit benefits from trellis support when fruiting. Excellent for subtropical SE Queensland.
varieties.availability: Stark Bro's, Four Winds Growers, Perfect Plants, widespread

varieties.name: Natchez
varieties.traits: Semi-erect, thornless, requires trellis, flexible canes

varieties.name: Hull
varieties.yield_notes: High, ripens slightly earlier than Chester, very winter hardy
varieties.growing_notes: Similar to Chester but earlier ripening. Suitable for humid subtropical climates with similar growing requirements. Processing quality berry.

varieties.name: Black Satin
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, mid-season, popular commercial variety
varieties.growing_notes: Performs in Humid subtropical subtropical climate. Large vigorous plant. Excellent for fresh eating and processing. Ripens early summer in SE Queensland.

varieties.name: Loch Ness
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, easy to grow

varieties.name: Prime-Ark Freedom
varieties.traits: Erect, thornless, world's first thornless primocane-fruiting, vigorous upright 1.5-1.8m
varieties.yield_notes: Two crops - primocanes fruit July, floricanes fruit June (earliest Arkansas variety), continues until frost

varieties.name: Prime-Ark Traveler
varieties.traits: Thornless, primocane-fruiting, vigorous upright, commercial release
varieties.yield_notes: High quality, two crops, primocanes ripen July, floricanes June

varieties.name: Prime-Ark Horizon
varieties.yield_notes: Very high especially on floricanes, good flavor, retains firmness in storage
varieties.growing_notes: Newer release suitable for humid subtropical climates. Limited leakage and decay. Good for subtropical production.

varieties.name: Sweet-Ark Ponca
varieties.yield_notes: High-yielding, good postharvest handling
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for humid subtropical climates. Early-market berry potential for shipping and local markets. Home garden use. Exceptional sweetness.

varieties.name: Kiowa
varieties.yield_notes: Very high 3.5-5.5kg per plant, longest harvest season 6+ weeks, ripens early June

varieties.name: Brazos
varieties.origin: Texas Agricultural Experiment Station, USA, 1959 (heirloom - 65+ years)
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, early ripening

varieties.name: Cherokee
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, early ripening over 25 days beginning June
varieties.growing_notes: Performs well in humid subtropical climates subtropical conditions. Oklahoma recommended. Good disease resistance.

varieties.name: Chickasaw
varieties.yield_notes: High, ripens with Shawnee
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for humid subtropical climates. Chill 500-700hr. Good disease resistance. Vigorous plant.

varieties.name: Rosborough
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive
varieties.growing_notes: Good for humid subtropical climates. Developed for Texas conditions (300hr chill). Recommended for eastern and south-central Texas climates. Heat adapted.

varieties.name: Boysenberry 🧬
varieties.origin: California, USA, 1920s-1935, Rudolph Boysen (heritage variety 90+ years)
varieties.traits: Trailing, requires trellis, thornless selections available, vigorous
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy yielding, late maturing

varieties.name: Youngberry 🧬
varieties.origin: Louisiana, USA, 1920s, Byrnes M Young (heritage - 100+ years)
varieties.yield_notes: Mid-season, Louisiana origin variety

varieties.name: Loganberry 🧬
varieties.origin: Santa Cruz, California, USA, 1881, James Harvey Logan (heritage - 144 years)
varieties.traits: Vining, trailing like blackberry parent, canes to 1.5m+, soft spines, hardy
varieties.yield_notes: 3.5-4kg per bush, 2 month fruiting midsummer-midautumn, fruits earlier than blackberry, 15 year lifespan
varieties.growing_notes: PERFORMS in humid subtropical climates. humid subtropical climates growers report success. Undisciplined growth requires training. Parent of boysenberry, olallieberry, marionberry. Historical significance as first major berry hybrid. Can self-propagate.

varieties.name: Tayberry
varieties.origin: Scottish Crop Research Institute, Scotland, 1979, named after River Tay
varieties.traits: Rambling habit 2m, thornless selections available, requires support
varieties.yield_notes: One large harvest July, abundant fruits can be heavy
varieties.growing_notes: May perform in humid subtropical climates but prefers cooler climates. Thornless makes harvesting easy. Excellent for jams, preserves, pie filling, fresh eating.

varieties.name: Marionberry
varieties.origin: Oregon State University USDA, USA, 1956, named after Marion County
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent, most commonly cultivated blackberry in Oregon

varieties.name: Olallieberry
varieties.origin: Oregon State University USDA, USA, 1950 released, 1935 developed
varieties.yield_notes: Productive, west coast variety
varieties.growing_notes: Marginal for humid subtropical climates - developed for Pacific coast. Parent of marionberry. Found mainly west coast US. Some humid subtropical climates growers may have success.

varieties.name: Jamun (Njaval)
varieties.origin: Native to India, China, Asia, Australia, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Nepal, Sri Lanka
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree 6-9m (can reach 30m), rough brown/gray bark, dense foliage, fast-growing ornamental, thrives in tropical monsoon
varieties.yield_notes: Fruits monsoon season, highly perishable (2 days room temp), doesn't ripen after harvest
varieties.growing_notes: PERFECT FOR tropical monsoon climates TROPICAL CLIMATE. Called 'Indian blackberry' due to color but completely different plant family. Thrives in tropical/subtropical moderate-high humidity. Prefers well-drained sandy loam or clayey soil rich in organic matter. Adapts to poor rocky waterlogged soils. Ideal temp 25-35°C. Full sun best. Drought-resistant once established. Rich in antioxidants, anti-diabetic, heart health, treats hemorrhoids, anemia. Deeply embedded in tropical monsoon climates culture and traditions. Available throughout monsoon at local markets.
varieties.availability: Widespread in tropical monsoon climates - local nurseries, markets, naturally occurring. Greens of tropical monsoon climates, local tree nurseries

varieties.name: Arapaho 🌱 (Low Chill Attempt)
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, earliest ripening
varieties.growing_notes: Marketed in tropical monsoon climates by Greens of tropical monsoon climates and other nurseries. Requires full sun minimum 6 hours, cool climate for flowering/fruiting, self-pollinating. May fruit weakly at higher tropical monsoon climates elevations or with afternoon shade. Susceptible to fungal diseases in monsoon humidity - requires disease management. Best chance of Rubus success in tropical monsoon climates but still challenging.
varieties.availability: Greens of tropical monsoon climates, iNetFarms tropical monsoon climates, Indian online nurseries

varieties.name: Kiowa 🌱 (Ultra-Low Chill Attempt)
varieties.traits: Primocane-fruiting (fruit on first-year canes), thornless, reduces chill dependency
varieties.yield_notes: Can fruit first year, extended season
varieties.growing_notes: Best chill-hour candidate for tropical monsoon climates due to 200hr requirement. May be attempted in tropical monsoon climates hills/higher elevations. Still requires some cool winter period which tropical monsoon climates lowlands lack. Thorny nature requires careful handling. Experimental only.
varieties.availability: May need import or specialized nurseries, not commonly available in tropical monsoon climates
